Object.Name: Hardware Watchdog
Object.Description1: This LwM2M Object provides monitoring and management of hardware watchdog timers commonly found in embedded systems and IoT devices. The watchdog is a hardware timer that must be periodically reset ("petted") to prevent system reboot. This object enables remote configuration and monitoring of watchdog behavior, particularly useful for OpenWRT-based routers and similar embedded Linux devices.

Item.Name: Watchdog Enable
Item.Description: Enable or disable the hardware watchdog. When enabled, the watchdog timer must be periodically reset to prevent system reboot. When disabled, the watchdog is stopped and will not trigger a reboot.

Item.Name: Timeout
Item.Description: The watchdog timeout period in seconds. If the watchdog is not reset within this time period, the hardware will trigger a system reboot. Typical values range from 5 to 60 seconds. The actual timeout may be rounded to the nearest value supported by the hardware.

Item.Name: Pet Interval
Item.Description: The interval at which the watchdog is automatically reset ("petted") by the system. This should be less than the timeout value. Typical values are 1/2 to 2/3 of the timeout period.

Item.Name: Status
Item.Description: Current watchdog status. Values: 0=Inactive (disabled), 1=Active (running normally), 2=Triggered (reboot in progress or imminent), 3=Error (watchdog device unavailable or malfunctioning).

Item.Name: Last Pet Time
Item.Description: Unix timestamp of the last successful watchdog reset operation. This can be used to verify that the watchdog is being serviced regularly.

Item.Name: Magic Close Enable
Item.Description: Enable the "magic close" feature. When enabled, writing a specific magic character ('V') to the watchdog device before closing allows proper shutdown of the watchdog. This is particularly relevant for OpenWRT systems using procd.

Item.Name: Pet Watchdog
Item.Description: Execute this resource to manually reset the watchdog timer. This operation writes to the watchdog device to prevent an imminent timeout.

Item.Name: Reset Watchdog
Item.Description: Execute this resource to reset the watchdog configuration to default values and restart the watchdog service.

Item.Name: Pet Count
Item.Description: Total number of successful watchdog reset operations since the last system boot or watchdog restart. This counter is useful for verifying watchdog activity and debugging.

Item.Name: Trigger Count
Item.Description: Number of times the watchdog has triggered a system reboot. This value is typically persisted across reboots to track system stability. A high value may indicate system instability or software issues.

Item.Name: Device Path
Item.Description: The file system path to the watchdog device, typically "/dev/watchdog" on Linux systems. This is informational and shows which hardware watchdog device is being used.

Item.Name: Hardware Timeout
Item.Description: The actual timeout value configured in the hardware watchdog device, as reported by the driver. This may differ from the requested timeout due to hardware limitations or driver rounding.

Item.Name: Boot Count
Item.Description: Total number of system boots detected. When combined with Trigger Count, this helps determine the ratio of watchdog-triggered reboots versus normal reboots.
